definitions.definition: (noun) Expectancy; condition of ownership of real property being undetermined; lapse in succession of ownership of estate, or title.; Suspension; temporary suppression; dormant condition.
(verb) To flee, often secretly; to steal away, particularly to avoid arrest or prosecution.; To withdraw from.
(adjective) Difficult to comprehend or understand.; Concealed or hidden out of the way; secret.
(noun) Address; greeting.; An attack. | (verb) To approach and speak to boldly or aggressively, as with a demand or request.; To join side to side; to border.
(noun) The top or highest point; pinnacle; culmination.; The crisis or height of a disease.

(verb) (provincial) To earn, earn by labor; earn money or one's living.; (provincial) To thrive or grow; to ripen. | (verb) To make addle; to grow addle; to muddle; To cause fertilised eggs to lose viability, by killing the developing embryo within through shaking, piercing, freezing or oiling, without breaking the shell. | (adjective) (of eggs) Bad, rotten; inviable, containing a dead embryo.; Confused; mixed up.
(verb) To bring forward or offer, as an argument, passage, or consideration which bears on a statement or case; to cite; to allege.
(noun) A needle-shaped peak.; An instrument for boring holes, used in blasting.
(adjective) Low in height; short.; Low in place or position. | (adjective) Of sound, a voice or an instrument, low in pitch or frequency.
(noun) A cheap showy ornament piece of jewellery; a gewgaw.; A club or sceptre carried by a jester.
(noun) A marketplace, particularly in the Middle East and South Asia, and often covered with shops and stalls.; A shop selling articles that are either exotic or eclectic.
(verb) To cause to become obscure or muddled.; (usually passive) To cover or surround with clouds.
(noun) A place or situation of chaotic uproar, and where confusion prevails.; An insane person; a lunatic; a madman.
(verb) To make (something) wet and limp, especially by dragging it along the ground. | (adjective) Wet and limp; unkempt.; Decaying, decrepit or dilapidated.
(verb) To fall upon; fall all over; overtake; To happen.
(verb) To be fit for
(adjective) Given to or marked by an ill-tempered nature; ill-tempered, cranky, surly, crabby.

(verb) To speak with the jargon of a class or subgroup.; To speak in set phrases. | (verb) To set (something) at an angle.; To give a sudden turn or new direction to. | (verb) To divide or parcel out. | (adjective) Having angles.; Inclined at an angle to something else; sloping.
(adjective) Having a lot of space inside; roomy.
(noun) An impulsive, seemingly unmotivated action, change of mind, or notion.; An unpredictable or sudden condition, change, or series of changes.
(noun) An impulsive, seemingly unmotivated action, change of mind, or notion.; An unpredictable or sudden condition, change, or series of changes.
(verb) To move rapidly straight ahead, especially in an uncontrolled way. | (noun) Rapid, uncontrollable headlong motion.
(noun) A set of bells, often in a bell tower, sometimes operated by means of a keyboard (manual or pedal), originating from the Low Countries.; A tune adapted to be played by musical bells.

(noun) (history) An immigrant from the Northern to the Southern States after the American Civil War of 1861–5, especially one who went South to gain political influence.; (by extension) One who comes to a place or organisation with which they have no previous connection with the sole or primary aim of personal gain, especially political or financial gain.
(noun) A walk that is taken regularly for good health and wellbeing.
(noun) The state or degree of being contented or satisfied.; Happiness in one's situation; satisfaction
(noun) A gradual healing after illness or injury.; The period of time spent healing.
(adjective) Material; tangible; physical.; Pertaining to the body; bodily; corporal.
(noun) A circle of people who associate with one another.; An exclusive group of people, who associate closely for a common purpose.
(verb) To linger; to stay; to tarry; To delay; to pause; to suspend proceedings or judgment in view of a doubt or difficulty; to hesitate; to put off the determination or conclusion of an affair.
(verb) To put down; to lay down; to deposit; to lay aside; to put away.; To remove (a leader) from (high) office, without killing the incumbent.
(noun) An act of consuming agricultural resources (crops, livestock), especially as plunder.; A raid or predatory attack.
(noun) Devilry.

(noun) The act of dissipating or dispersing; a state of dispersion or separation; dispersion; waste.; A dissolute course of life, in which health, money, etc., are squandered in pursuit of pleasure; profuseness in immoral indulgence, as late hours, riotous living, etc.; dissoluteness.
(adjective) Unrestrained by morality.; Recklessly abandoned to sensual pleasures.

(noun) A viral disease of animals, such as dogs and cats, characterised by fever, coughing and catarrh.; A disorder of the humours of the body; a disease. | (verb) To temper or mix unduly; to make disproportionate; to change the due proportions of.; To derange the functions of, whether bodily, mental, or spiritual; to disorder; to disease.
(verb) To extend or expand, as from internal pressure; to swell; To extend; to stretch out; to spread out. | (adjective) Extended or expanded, as from internal pressure; swollen.; Gravid.

(noun) A Muslim council of state, specifically that of viziers of the Ottoman Empire that discussed and recommended new laws and law changes to a higher authority (the sultan).; The council chamber where this court is held; (by extension), any court of justice.
(verb) (clothing) To remove or take off, especially of clothing.; To remove or tip a hat, as in greeting, salutation or as a mark of respect.
(verb) To pursue with the intent to catch.; To follow in an annoying or harassing way.
(adverb) In a way that is stubbornly persistent.; Sullenly, gloomily

(verb) To use words of equivocal or doubtful signification; to express one's opinions in terms which admit of different senses, with intent to deceive; to use ambiguous expressions with a view to mislead; as, to equivocate is the work of duplicity.; To render equivocal or ambiguous.

(noun) A logical fallacy resulting from the use of multiple meanings of a single expression.; The use of expressions susceptible of a double signification, possibly intentionally and with the aim of misleading.
(adjective) Sensitive to sexual arousal.; Causing sexual arousal; erotogenic.
(adjective) Former, previous.; Respected, honourable. | (adverb) Formerly; in the past.
(noun) The act of belching, of expelling gas from the stomach through the mouth.; An erumpent blast of gas, wind, or other matter ejected from the depths of the earth.
(noun) A learned or scholarly person | (adjective) Learned, scholarly, with emphasis on knowledge gained from books.
(verb) To avoid; to shun, to shy away from.
(noun) The act or practice of exhorting; the act of inciting to laudable deeds; incitement to that which is good or commendable.; Language intended to incite and encourage
(noun) A flood resulting from heavy rain or a spring thaw.; A small stream, especially one flowing into the sea.
(adjective) Easily broken into small fragments, crumbled, or reduced to powder.; (of soil) Loose and large-grained in consistency.
(noun) A sudden surge of excitement.; A shiver, a thrill.
(noun) The support about which a lever pivots.; A crux or pivot; a central point.

(noun) A person who sells, makes, repairs, alters, cleans, or otherwise deals in clothing made of fur.; A person who secures accommodation for an army. | (adjective) Covered with fur, or with something resembling fur.; Of or related to the furry subculture.
(noun) A trench cut in the soil, as when plowed in order to plant a crop.; Any trench, channel, or groove, as in wood or metal. | (verb) To cut one or more grooves in (the ground, etc.).; To wrinkle.
(noun) A type of woolen cloth with a diagonal ribbed texture on one side.; A similar fabric, made from cotton.
(adverb) Merrily.; Showily.

(noun) Bile, especially that of an animal; the greenish, profoundly bitter-tasting fluid found in bile ducts and gall bladders, structures associated with the liver.; The gall bladder. | (verb) To bother or trouble.; To harass, to harry, often with the intent to cause injury.
(adjective) Brave, valiant.; Honorable.
(noun) A witch.; A spiteful and violent person, especially a woman.
(noun) A small hill.
(noun) The writing of history; a written history.; The study of the discipline and practice of history and the writings of past historians.
(adverb) Up to this or that time.
(adjective) Angry, annoyed, indignant or irritated.; Easily offended; thin-skinned or touchy.
(noun) A platform where candidates in an election give speeches; a husting.; (by extension) An election campaign.
(noun) An idea dominating the mind and maintained despite evidence to the contrary; (loosely), an obsession.; A recurring musical theme; a leitmotif.
(adjective) Mutually destructive; most often applied to warfare.; Characterized by struggle within a group, usually applied to an ethnic or familial relationship.
(verb) To suggest or disclose (something) discreetly.; To notify.
(noun) The act of intimating.; The thing intimated.
(noun) The act of intimating.; The thing intimated.
(verb) To cause someone to become accustomed to something (usually) unpleasant.; To take effect, to be operative.
(verb) (with against or occasionally about, formerly also with on, at, upon) To complain loudly, to give voice to one's censure or criticism; To draw in or away; to entice, inveigle.
(adjective) Lacking respect; not having or not showing respect for or seriousness towards something that is usually treated with respect; going against conventional precepts.

(noun) A juvenile male turkey.; Police, a federal government officer. | (verb) To play a Discordian prank on (somebody), involving a large number of people sending bizarre letters or parcels to that person. | (adjective) Adequate; satisfactory; acceptable.
(noun) A master-at-arms onboard a Royal Navy ship | (adjective) Airy; showy; finical.; (by extension) Characterized by an affected or fantastical manner.
(adjective) Humorous, amusing or joking.

(noun) A step formed in material by two adjacent reverse bends.; A notch or tooth in the joining surface of any piece of building material to prevent slipping. | (verb) To shake slightly; to push suddenly but slightly, so as to cause to shake or totter; to jostle; to jog.; To shake or totter; to slip out of place.
(adjective) Lacking enthusiasm, energy, or strength; drooping or flagging from weakness, fatigue, or lack of energy; Heavy; dull; dragging; wanting spirit or animation; listless; apathetic.

(noun) A state of the body or mind caused by exhaustion or disease and characterized by a languid or weary feeling; lassitude; an instance of this.; Melancholy caused by lovesickness, sadness, etc.; an instance of this.

(adjective) Tastelessly gaudy; superficially attractive but having in reality no value or substance; falsely alluring.; Involving unlawful sexual connection or lack of consent by at least one party (said of a romantic relationship)

(noun) Any of several deciduous trees of the genus Prosopis found in North America, and used as forage, which have long, beige seed/bean pods which may be dried and ground into a sweet, nutty flour.; The wood of these trees, used for smoking food, or charcoal made from this wood.
(verb) To measure.; (usually with “out”) To dispense, measure (out), allot (especially punishment, reward etc.). | (noun) The act of one who metes; a distribution or handing out.
(noun) A quality of endurance and courage.; Good temperament and character.
(noun) Demeanor; facial expression or attitude, especially one which is intended by its bearer.; A specific facial expression.

(noun) A person who is trained to preach, to perform religious ceremonies, and to afford pastoral care at a Protestant church.; A politician who heads a ministry (national or regional government department for public service). | (verb) To attend to (the needs of); to tend; to take care (of); to give aid; to give service.; To function as a clergyman or as the officiant in church worship
(adjective) Filled with mirth.
(noun) A person who hoards money rather than spending it; one who is cheap or extremely parsimonious.; A kind of earth auger, typically large-bored and often hand-operated.
(noun) A modest, small, or trifling amount.
(verb) To mark with blotches of different color, or shades of color, as if stained; to spot; to maculate. | (adjective) Colored in patches; spotted

(adjective) Emerging; just coming into existence.; Describing a quantity of object that is starting to grow from zero or an infinitesimal beginning. Also the creation or identification of an infinitesimal delta.
(verb) To harden; to obdure. | (adjective) Stubbornly persistent, generally in wrongdoing; refusing to reform or repent.; Physically hardened, toughened.
(adjective) Obedient; compliant with someone else's orders or wishes.; Excessively eager and attentive to please or to obey instructions; fawning, subservient, servile.
(noun) The internal organs of an animal, used as animal food.; A by-product of the grain milling process, which may include bran, husks, etc.

(verb) To kill.; To switch off. | (noun) The area of the sea in which a ship can be seen in the distance from land, excluding the parts nearest the shore, and beyond the anchoring ground.; The distance that a ship at sea keeps away from land, often because of navigational dangers, fog and other hazards; a position at a distance from shore.
(adjective) Appearing weak, pale or wan.
(noun) Paleness; want of color; pallidity; wanness.
(noun) A formal speech or opus publicly praising someone or something.; Someone who writes or delivers such a speech. | (adjective) Panegyrical
(noun) A person of preeminent qualities, who acts as a pattern or model for others.; A companion; a match; an equal.
(noun) A low protective wall.; Part of a perimeter that extends above the roof.
(adjective) Pertaining to a parish.; Characterized by an unsophisticated focus on local concerns to the exclusion of wider contexts; elementary in scope or outlook.
(noun) A random or sudden outburst (of activity).; An explosive event during a volcanic eruption.
(noun) A random or sudden outburst (of activity).; An explosive event during a volcanic eruption.

(noun) The act of providing approval and support; backing; championship.; Customers collectively; clientele; business. | (verb) To support by being a patron of.; To be a regular customer or client of; to patronize
(noun) One who is extremely poor.; One living on or eligible for public charity.
(noun) An excessive attention to detail or rules.; An overly ambitious display of learning.
(adjective) Characterized by exceptionally early development or maturity.; Exhibiting advanced skills and aptitudes at an abnormally early age.
(verb) To pin; fasten. | (verb) (of birds) To groom; to trim or dress with the beak, as the feathers.; To show off, posture, or smarm. | (noun) A grooming or posturing.
(noun) A warning of a future event; an omen.; An intuition of a future event; a presentiment. | (verb) To predict or foretell something.; To make a prediction.
(noun) A tinker.; A petty thief or pickpocket. | (verb) To filch or steal.; To ride

(noun) The state of being deprived of or lacking an attribute formerly or properly possessed; the loss or absence of such an attribute.; The state of being very poor, and lacking the basic necessities of life.
(noun) Any of various shrubs and small trees in the genus Ligustrum.
(noun) Offspring or descendants considered as a group.; Descent, lineage, ancestry.
(adjective) Favorable; benevolent.; Advantageous.
(noun) The particular character or essence of someone or something; individuality.; A characteristic; an attribute.
(adjective) Pertaining to or having the characteristics of prose.; (of writing or speaking) Straightforward; matter-of-fact; lacking the feeling or elegance of poetry.
(verb) To forbid or prohibit.; To denounce.

(noun) A state of dreaming while awake; a loose or irregular train of thought; musing or meditation; daydream.; An extravagant conceit of the imagination; a vision. | (noun) A caper, a frolic; merriment.
(verb) To attack (someone) with abusive language. | (noun) Reproach; abuse; vilification
(noun) A form of theatrical entertainment in which recent events, popular fads, etc., are parodied. Any entertainment featuring skits, dances, and songs.
(noun) Any disorder of the muscles, tendons, joints, bones, nerves, characterized by pain, discomfort and disability.; Atrophic arthritis, rheumatoid arthritis
(adjective) Of, relating to, or producing rheum from the mucous membranes; watery
(noun) Ice formed by the rapid freezing of cold water droplets of fog on to a cold surface.; A coating or sheet of ice so formed. | (verb) To freeze or congeal into hoarfrost.
(adverb) In a rueful manner; causing, feeling or expressing regret or sorrow.
(noun) A loser or a fool.; A person who is clumsy or who hurts others emotionally.

(verb) To give off sparks; to shine as if emanating sparks; to twinkle or glow.; To throw off like sparks. | (adjective) That scintillates with brief flashes of light; sparkling.; Brilliantly or impressively clever, exciting, amusing or witty.
(noun) A descendant, especially a first-generation descendant of a distinguished family.; The heir to a throne.
(adjective) Of a person: diligent in application or pursuit; constant and persevering in business or in endeavours to effect a goal; steadily industrious.; Of an activity: carried out with diligence.

(noun) A substance that causes sepsis or putrefaction.; A septic tank; a system for the disposal of sewage into a septic tank, a septic system. | (adjective) Of or pertaining to sepsis.; Causing sepsis or putrefaction.
(noun) A burial chamber.; A recess in some early churches in which the reserved sacrament, etc. were kept from Good Friday till Easter. | (verb) To place in a sepulchre.
(noun) A large bronze or (rarely) small silver coin minted during the Roman Republic and Empire, valued at two and a half asses (a quarter of a denarius).
(noun) A sexy person.
(verb) To walk while shuffling or dragging the feet. | (noun) An awkward, irregular gait. | (adjective) Who walks while dragging or shuffling the feet.
(noun) A type of wooden board that has rabbets to allow them to be overlapped.
(noun) A young, newly-weaned pig. | (noun) A geep, a sheep-goat hybrid (whether artificially produced or the result of animals from these species naturally intermating).
(noun) An act of sidling. | (verb) To (cause something to) move sideways.; In the intransitive sense often followed by up: to (cause something to) advance in a coy, furtive, or unobtrusive manner.

(noun) (grammar) In Latin and other languages: a type of verbal noun used in the ablative and accusative cases, which shares the same stem as the passive participle.; (grammar) In Swedish: a verb form that combines with an inflection of ha to form the present perfect and pluperfect tenses. | (adjective) Lying on its back.; Reluctant to take action due to indifference or moral weakness; apathetic or passive towards something.
(verb) To humble oneself before (another) in making a request; to beg or beseech.; To entreat for; to ask for earnestly and humbly.
(noun) An act of supplicating; a humble request.; A prayer or entreaty to a god.
(noun) Something that is supposed; an assumption made to account for known facts, conjecture.; The act or an instance of supposing.
(adjective) Irritated, bad-tempered, unfriendly.; Threatening, menacing, gloomy. | (adverb) In an arrogant or supercilious manner.
(noun) A light horse-drawn carriage with forward-facing seats accommodating two or four people, popular in the United States; a motorized carriage of similar design.
(noun) A swarthy person. | (adjective) Tawny, dusky, dark.; Dark-skinned.
(noun) A state of indecision or confusion. | (verb) To be indecisive or in a state of confusion; to dither.
(noun) One who resides in the woods.; A fabled deity of the wood; a faun, a satyr. | (adjective) Pertaining to the forest, or woodlands.; Residing in a forest or wood.
(adjective) Silent; temperamentally untalkative; disinclined to speak.

(noun) The sense of touch; feeling.; The stroke in beating time. | (verb) To use a tact (a kind of verbal operant; see noun sense). | (noun) A maneuver, or action calculated to achieve some end.; A maneuver used against an enemy.
(noun) A light brown to brownish orange colour. | (adjective) Of a light brown to brownish orange color.; A sweet, fortified wine which is blended and matured in wood.
(noun) A storm, especially one with severe winds.; Any violent tumult or commotion. | (verb) To storm.; To disturb, as by a tempest.
(adjective) (by extension) Of speech or style: brief, concise, to the point.; (by extension) Of manner or speech: abruptly or brusquely short; curt.

(noun) Any doctrine of religious philosophy and mysticism claiming that knowledge of God can be attained through mystical insight and spiritual ecstasy, and that direct communication with the transcendent world is possible.; Any system which claims to attain communication with God and superior spirits by physical processes.
(adjective) (of cloth) shabby, frayed and worn to an extent that warp threads show; Damaged or shabby
(adjective) Fearful; afraid; timid
(adjective) Stylish, high-toned, upscale.
(noun) (Oxford University slang) An inferior racing boat, or one who rows in such a boat. | (adjective) Unmoving; Dormant or hibernating
(noun) A state of being inactive or stuporous.; A state of apathy or lethargy.
(adjective) (of people) Capable of being easily led, taught, or managed.; (of a problem) Easy to deal with or manage
(verb) To walk in a messy or unattractively casual way; to trail through dirt.; To walk about, especially when expending much effort, or unnecessary effort. | (noun) Laborious walking.
(noun) The transcending, or going beyond, empiricism, and ascertaining a priori the fundamental principles of human knowledge.; Ambitious and imaginative vagueness in thought, imagery, or diction.

(noun) Arduous or painful exertion; excessive labor, suffering, hardship.; Specifically, the labor of childbirth. | (noun) The act of traveling; passage from place to place.; (in the plural) A series of journeys. | (noun) A traditional North American Indian sled-like vehicle, pulled by person, dog, or horse.

(noun) Arduous or painful exertion; excessive labor, suffering, hardship.; Specifically, the labor of childbirth. | (verb) To toil.; To go through the labor of childbirth. | (noun) The act of traveling; passage from place to place.; (in the plural) A series of journeys. | (noun) A traditional North American Indian sled-like vehicle, pulled by person, dog, or horse.
(adjective) Not sullied.
(adjective) (of a man) Courteous, polite, refined, and suave.
(verb) To seize power from another, usually by illegitimate means.; To use and assume the coat of arms of another person. | (noun) Usurpation

(verb) To sway unsteadily from one side to the other; oscillate.; To swing indecisively from one course of action or opinion to another. | (noun) Vacillation | (adjective) Liable to vacillate; wavering, irresolute.

(verb) To add variety to something.; To change the appearance of something, especially by covering with patches or streaks of different colour. | (adjective) Streaked, spotted, or otherwise marked with a variety of color.; (by extension) Very colorful.
(adjective) Venous; pertaining to veins.
(noun) The greenness of lush or growing vegetation; also: the vegetation itself.; (hence) A condition of health and vigour. | (verb) To cover with verdure.
(noun) The property of seeming true, of resembling reality; resemblance to reality, realism.; A statement which merely appears to be true.
(noun) Truth, fact or reality, especially an enduring religious or ethical truth.; A true statement; an established doctrine.

(noun) A robe, gown, or other article of clothing worn as an indication of office.; Any of the special articles of clothing worn by members of the clergy etc., especially a garment worn at the celebration of the Eucharist.

(noun) A room in a church where the clergy put on their vestments and where these are stored; also used for meetings and classes; a sacristy.; A committee of parishioners elected to administer the temporal affairs of a parish.

(noun) The yield of grapes or wine from a vineyard or district during one season.; Wine, especially high-quality, identified as to year and vineyard or district of origin. | (verb) To harvest (grapes).; To make (wine) from grapes. | (adjective) Of or relating to a vintage, or to wine identified by a specific vintage.; Having an enduring appeal; high-quality.
(noun) Countenance; appearance; one's face.
(adverb) In a vociferous manner.

(noun) Any of certain canids called foxes (including the true foxes, the arctic fox and the grey fox); distinguished from the canines, which are regarded as similar to the dog and wolf.; A person considered vulpine (cunning); a fox. | (adjective) Pertaining to a fox.; Having the characteristics of a fox, foxlike; cunning.

(noun) A pampered or coddled person.; An overly playful person; a trifler. | (verb) To rove and ramble without restraint, rule, or limit; to revel; to play loosely; to frolic.; To waste or squander, especially in pleasure (most often with away). | (adjective) Undisciplined, unruly; not able to be controlled.; Playful, sportive; merry or carefree.
(noun) A general confusion or muddle.; A tossing or rolling about. | (verb) To roll around; to wallow.; To revel, luxuriate.
(verb) To hone or rub on with some substance, as a piece of stone, for the purpose of sharpening – see whetstone.; To stimulate or make more keen. | (noun) The process by which something is whetted.
(verb) To wuther. | (adverb) To what place.
(noun) A chronic or heavy drinker of cheap wine or other alcohol; a drunk or drunkard.; A wine enthusiast; an oenophile. | (noun) A hypothetical particle that is the superpartner of the W boson.
(noun) An item of clothing made from wool | (adjective) Made of wool.; Of or relating to wool or woolen cloths.
(noun) Yarn made from long strands of wool.; The fine, smooth fabric made from such wool yarn.
(adverb) In a wry manner.
A place in the state of New York where people go over in the summer for vacation to enjoy artistic events.
A kind of travelling tent-show which used to move across America featuring popular talks.
To sour or curdle.
Thickened or curdled.
definitions.example: The milk became clabbered.

definitions.definition: To foretell (something), especially by the use of divination.
To guess or discover (something) through intuition or insight.
To search for (underground objects or water) using a divining rod.
The action of the verb to divine in any sense.
That or who divines (in any sense of the verb).
An expression of praise to God, especially a short hymn sung as part of a Christian worship service.
Of or suggesting Eden, the paradise of the Bible.
A non-Jewish person.
(grammar) A noun derived from a proper noun which denotes something belonging to or coming from a particular city, nation, or country.
Non-Jewish.
Heathen, pagan.
Relating to a clan, tribe, or nation; clannish, tribal, national.
Using as few words as possible; pithy and concise.
Attempting to achieve goals by cunning, scheming, and unscrupulous methods, especially in politics or in advancing one's career.
Related to the philosophical system of Niccolò Machiavelli.
A mild rebuke, or an implied criticism.
Disgrace or shame.
An object of scorn.
To criticize or rebuke (someone).
To disgrace, or bring shame upon.
A fine-grained white or lightly-tinted variety of gypsum, used ornamentally.
A variety of calcite, translucent and sometimes banded.
An off-white colour, like that of alabaster.
Made of alabaster
definitions.example: The crown is stored in an alabaster box with an onyx handle and a gold lock.

definitions.definition: Resembling alabaster: white, pale, translucent.
definitions.example: An ominous alabaster fog settled in the valley.

definitions.definition: (with from) To get off or exit a vehicle or animal; to descend; to dismount.
definitions.example: He alighted from his horse.

definitions.definition: (with on or at) To descend and settle, lodge, rest, or stop.
definitions.example: A flying bird alights on a tree.

definitions.definition: (followed by upon) To find by accident; to come upon.
To make light or less heavy; lighten; alleviate.
To light; light up; illuminate.
To set light to; light.
Alleviation; abatement; check.
An alloy.
To make quiet or put at rest; to pacify or appease; to quell; to calm.
definitions.example: to allay popular excitement

definitions.definition: To alleviate; to abate; to mitigate.
definitions.example: to allay the severity of affliction or the bitterness of adversity

definitions.definition: To subside, abate, become peaceful.
The round walkway encircling the altar in many cathedrals.
Any part of a building intended for walking in; a corridor.
Of, relating to, or adapted to walking
definitions.example: ambulatory exercise

definitions.definition: Able to walk about and not bedridden.
definitions.example: an ambulatory patient

definitions.definition: Performed on or involving an ambulatory patient or an outpatient.
definitions.example: ambulatory medical care

definitions.definition: To make better, or improve, something perceived to be in a negative condition.
definitions.example: They offered some compromises in an effort to ameliorate the situation.

definitions.definition: To become better; improve.
Friendly; kind; sweet; gracious
definitions.example: amiable ideas

definitions.definition: Of a pleasant and likeable nature; kind-hearted; easy to like
definitions.example: an amiable person

definitions.definition: Erroneous in date; containing an anachronism; in a wrong time; not applicable to or not appropriate for the time.
definitions.example: If you know where to look in the movie, you can spot an anachronistic wrist watch on one of the Roman soldiers.

definitions.definition: (of a person) Having opinions from the past; preferring things or values of the past; behind the times; overly conservative.
Straight away; at once.
Soon; in a little while.
At another time; then; again.
An anonymous person, especially an author.
A work with an unknown author.
A work without a title.
Anonymous
A feeling of dislike (normally towards someone, less often towards something); repugnance or distaste.
Natural contrariety or incompatibility
definitions.example: oil and water have antipathy

definitions.definition: A collector or student of, or expert in, antiquities or antiques.

definitions.definition: To self-inflict real injury or infection (to inflict self-harm) in order to avoid work, obligation, or perilous risk.
In a manful manner; with the characteristics considered typical of a man, such as strength, courage, and determination.
A trough for animals to eat from.
A mansard roof
The upper storey of a building, surrounded by such a roof
(of a roof) having two slopes on each side, the lower being steeper than the upper
A puppet, usually made of wood, which is animated by the pulling of strings.
The buffel duck.
The stomach, especially of an animal.
The upper digestive tract (where food enters the body), especially the mouth and jaws of a fearsome and ravenous creature.
Any large, insatiable or perilous opening.
Mother.
A gull.
Black bile, formerly thought to be one of the four "cardinal humours" of animal bodies.
Great sadness or depression, especially of a thoughtful or introspective nature.
Affected with great sadness or depression.
definitions.example: Melancholy people don't talk much.

definitions.definition: Causing much harm in a subtle way.
Causing death or injury; deadly.
Highly injurious or destructive to life: deadly.
Annoying.
Harmful to morals or public order.
(plural phalanxes) An ancient Greek and Macedonian military unit that consisted of several ranks and files (lines) of soldiers in close array with joined shields and long spears.
(historical sociology) A Fourierite utopian community; a phalanstery.
(plural phalanxes) A large group of people, animals or things, compact or closely massed, or tightly knit and united in common purpose.
Study of set or fixed expressions.
The style in which words and phrases are used in writing or speech.
A group of specialized words and expressions used by a particular group.
The art or pseudoscience of deducing the predominant temper and other characteristic qualities of the mind from the outward appearance, especially from the features of the face.
The face or countenance, with respect to the temper of the mind; particular configuration, cast, or expression of countenance, as denoting character.
The art of telling fortunes by inspection of the features.
A feeling of enmity; ill-feeling, animosity; a transient feeling of wounded pride.
A feeling of irritation or resentment, awakened by a social slight or injury; offence, especially taken in an emotional sense with little thought or consideration.
Keenly felt desire; a longing.
To wound the pride of; to excite to anger.
To take pride in; to pride oneself on.
To stimulate (a feeling, emotion); to offend by slighting; to excite (someone) to action by causing resentment or jealousy.
definitions.example: I believe this will pique your interest.

definitions.definition: In piquet, the right of the elder hand to count thirty in hand, or to play before the adversary counts one.
To score a pique against.
A chigger or jigger, Tunga penetrans.
A durable ribbed fabric made from cotton, rayon, or silk.
To fold; to double in narrow folds; to pleat
definitions.example: to plait a ruffle

definitions.definition: To interweave the strands or locks of; to braid
definitions.example: plaiting rope

definitions.definition: Any representation of part of a sphere on a plane surface
Any of several charts of the celestial sphere having an overlay or window that may be adjusted to show the stars visible at a particular time, or from a particular place
A network or interwoven mass, especially of nerves, blood vessels, or lymphatic vessels.
The system of equations required for the complete expression of the relations which exist between a set of quantities.
A block or slab upon which a column, pedestal, statue or other structure is based.
The bottom course of stones or bricks supporting a wall.
A base or pedestal beneath a cabinet.
A riot aimed at persecution or massacre of a particular ethnic or religious group, usually Jews.
An antisemitic hate crime with a large death toll, irrespective of the number of perpetrators.
To preside as a bishop, especially at mass.
To act like a pontiff; to express one's position or opinions dogmatically and pompously as if they were absolutely correct.
To speak in a patronizing, supercilious or pompous manner, especially at length.
Of momentous or ominous significance.
Ominously prophetic.
Puffed up with vanity.
Something that portends an event about to occur, especially an unfortunate or evil event; an omen.
A portending; significance
definitions.example: a howl of dire portent

definitions.definition: Something regarded as portentous; a marvel; prodigy.
A soft, moist mass applied topically to a sore, aching or lesioned part of the body to soothe. A poultice is usually wrapped in cloth and often warmed before being applied.
To treat with a poultice.
A teacher or tutor.
The head of a preceptory of Knights Templar.
A doctor who gives practical training to medical students, nurses etc.
A rule or principle, especially one governing personal conduct.
A written command, especially a demand for payment.
An order issued by one local authority to another specifying the rate of tax to be charged on its behalf.
A female protector.
A region of the earth or of a continent; a district or country.
An administrative subdivision of certain countries, including Canada and China.
(Roman history) An area outside Italy which is administered by a Roman governor.
The quality or state of being prudent; wisdom in the way of caution and provision; discretion; carefulness; hence, also, economy; frugality.
Uneasy with desire; itching; especially, having a lascivious anxiety or propensity; lustful.
Arousing or appealing to sexual desire.
Curious, especially inappropriately so.
Any of various amines formed by putrefactive bacteria.
Food poisoning.
A raised platform in a church, usually enclosed, where the minister or preacher stands when giving the sermon.

Activity performed from a church pulpit, in other words, preaching, sermons, religious teaching, the preaching profession, preachers collectively or an individual preaching position; by extension: bully pulpit.
A raised desk, lectern, or platform for an orator or public speaker.
To take the property of another, often in breach of trust; to appropriate wrongfully; to steal.
To commit theft; to thieve.
The swede, or Swedish turnip; the European plant Brassica napus var. napobrassica
The edible root of this plant
A white, crystalline powder, C7H5NO3S, used as an artificial sweetener in food products
Sentimentalism
Of or relating to sugar.
Excessively sweet in action or disposition; syrupy.
Sentimental or romantic to the point of ridiculousness.
Of or relating to saccharin.
Keen sense of smell.
The quality of being sage, wise, or able to make good decisions; the quality of being perceptive, astute or insightful.
A large hall or reception room.
An open pavilion in Thailand used as a meeting place or to shelter from the weather.
To become sallow.
To cause (someone or something) to become sallow.
(of skin) Yellowish.
(of a person) Having skin (especially on the face) of a sickly pale colour.
(of objects or dim light) Having a similar pale, yellowish colour.
A European willow, Salix caprea, that has broad leaves, large catkins and tough wood.
A willow twig or branch.
A willow
Any tree that looks like a willow
An object made from the above trees' wood
A sortie of troops from a besieged place against an enemy.
A sudden rushing forth.
definitions.example: Flocks of these birds stir up flying insects, which can then be picked off in quick sallies.

definitions.definition: A witty statement or quip, usually at the expense of one's interlocutor.
To make a sudden attack (e.g. on an enemy from a defended position).
definitions.example: A feeding strategy of some birds is to sally out from a perch to snatch an insect and then returning to the same or a different perch.

definitions.definition: To set out on an excursion; venture; depart (often followed by "forth.")
definitions.example: As she sallied forth from her boudoir, you would never have guessed how quickly she could strip for action. - William Manchester

definitions.definition: To venture off the beaten path.
A member of the Salvation Army.
A kind of stonefly.
A wren.
Promoting health or well-being; wholesome, especially as related to air.
A bloodthirsty person.
The plant common yarrow, or herba sanguinaria (Achillea millefolium).
(of an event) Involving bloodshed.
(of a person) Eager to shed blood; bloodthirsty.
(of an object) Consisting of, covered with, or similar in appearance to blood.
Scornfully mocking or cynical.
definitions.example: He distances himself from people with his nasty, sardonic laughter.

definitions.definition: Disdainfully or ironically humorous.
To satisfy (thirst, or other desires).
To cool (something) with water or another liquid.
To become mixed with water, so that a true chemical combination takes place.
definitions.example: The lime slakes.

definitions.definition: To besmear.
Allayed; quenched; extinguished
definitions.example: a slaked thirst

definitions.definition: Mixed with water so that a true chemical combination has taken place
To rotate or turn something about its axis.
To veer a vehicle.
To insert extra ticks or skip some ticks of a clock to slowly correct its time.
Somewhat drunk; tipsy.
The skin shed by a snake or other reptile.
definitions.example: That is the slough of a rattler; we must be careful.

definitions.definition: Dead skin on a sore or ulcer.
definitions.example: This is the slough that came off of his skin after the burn.

definitions.definition: To shed (skin).
definitions.example: Snakes slough their skin periodically.

definitions.definition: To slide off (like a layer of skin).
definitions.example: A week after he was burned, a layer of skin on his arm sloughed off.

definitions.definition: To discard.
definitions.example: East sloughed a heart.

definitions.definition: A muddy or marshy area.
(Eastern United States) A type of swamp or shallow lake system, typically formed as or by the backwater of a larger waterway, similar to a bayou with trees.
definitions.example: We paddled under a canopy of trees through the slough.

definitions.definition: (Western United States) A secondary channel of a river delta, usually flushed by the tide.
An artificial passage for water, fitted with a valve or gate, for example in a canal lock or a mill stream, for stopping or regulating the flow.
A water gate or floodgate.
Hence, an opening or channel through which anything flows; a source of supply.
To make solemn, or official, through ceremony or legal act.
definitions.example: The couple chose to solemnize their relationship in a secular ceremony, instead of having a wedding.

definitions.definition: To make grave, serious, and reverential.
The state of being solicitous; uneasiness of mind occasioned by fear of evil or desire for good; anxiety.
Special or pronounced concern or attention.
A cause of anxiety or concern.
The act of a character speaking to themselves so as to reveal their thoughts to the audience.
definitions.example: At the end of the second act the main villain gave a soliloquy detailing his plans to attack the protagonist.

definitions.definition: (authorship) A speech or written discourse in this form.
(very rare) To issue a soliloquy.
One who tells the truth; a truthful person
One who predicts the future, using magic, intuition or intelligence; a diviner
A mantis or rearhorse
A method of teaching using the techniques of philosophy and rhetoric.
A flawed argument, superficially correct in its reasoning, usually designed to deceive.
An intentional fallacy.
Something inducing sleep, especially a drug.
definitions.example: The doctor prescribed a soporific to help the patient sleep.

definitions.definition: Something boring or dull.
(pharmaceutical effect) Tending to induce sleep.
Boring, dull.
definitions.example: The professor delivered a soporific lecture.

definitions.definition: Distasteful, ignoble, vile, or contemptible.
Dirty or squalid.
Morally degrading.
Something kept or steeped in brine
The act of sousing; a plunging into water.
A person suffering from alcoholism.
To immerse in liquid; to steep or drench.
To steep in brine; to pickle.
The act of sousing, or swooping.
A heavy blow.
To strike, beat.
To fall heavily.
To pounce upon.
A sou (the French coin).
A small amount.
To turn over soil with a spade to loosen the ground for planting.
The act by which soil is spaded, or turned over by digging.
Not invited, requested or asked for
Unfamiliar, strange, foreign.
Clumsy, awkward.
Unrefined, crude.
Consistently accurate; not missing a target.
Unpretentious, real, genuine
Incessant; never slackening
A piece of hand luggage such as a suitcase or travelling bag.
